mnemonics: P is Not Always Computationally Trivial
keyPoints: A decision tree can solve a problem by trying all possible inputs, but its time complexity is exponential, making it impractical for large inputs.
NP problems are a class of problems where no known polynomial-time algorithm exists; they are often used to model real-world problems.
NP-complete problems are a subset of NP problems, where a solution to one problem can be used to solve other NP problems; they are considered the hardest problems in NP.
